task_metadata.current_task: Implement in Python using collections.deque for efficient window management, re module for regex matching, and assume logs are line-based with timestamps parseable as floats or ISO8601
task_metadata.status: Implementing core algorithm in Python with standard libraries

nodes.description: Decompose the problem into core components: regex pattern matching on log lines, time-windowing logic, and threshold-based filtering (K matches within N seconds)
nodes.rationale: Understanding the requirements shows three distinct challenges: (1) pattern matching individual lines, (2) temporal aggregation/windowing, and (3) filtering based on count thresholds. Breaking these down helps identify algorithms and data structures needed.
nodes.task_state: Planning core algorithm and data structures for log filtering solution

nodes.description: Need an efficient algorithm to check multiple overlapping N-second time windows across large log files without quadratic complexity
nodes.rationale: Naively comparing every log entry against every N-second window would be O(n²) in the worst case. A sliding window or event-based approach is needed for reasonable performance on large logs.

nodes.description: Choose a sliding window algorithm: iterate through matched log entries in chronological order, maintain a deque of entries within the current N-second window, check if deque size >= K
nodes.rationale: Sliding window is O(n) for n log entries—each entry is added and removed from the window exactly once. This mitigates the performance constraint. The deque efficiently tracks entries by timestamp, allowing us to drop expired entries and count active ones.
nodes.task_state: Designing sliding window algorithm to process matched log entries

nodes.description: Implement in Python using collections.deque for efficient window management, re module for regex matching, and assume logs are line-based with timestamps parseable as floats or ISO8601
nodes.rationale: Python is suitable for log processing tasks; deque provides O(1) append/popleft operations. The re module is standard and efficient. Timestamp parsing handles the temporal aspect required for windowing.
nodes.task_state: Implementing core algorithm in Python with standard libraries

edges.rationale: The performance constraint directly shapes the algorithm choice—the decomposition must account for the need to avoid quadratic complexity in windowing logic
The sliding window approach resolves the performance constraint by achieving O(n) complexity instead of quadratic, making it suitable for large log files
